ground_truth.title: Indiana Judicial Branch: Self-Service Legal Center: Getting Legal Help
ground_truth.main_content: Court and clerk's office staff—whether or not they are licensed to practice law—cannot give legal advice because they have to stay neutral in every case. The Self-Service Legal Center provides resources to help you find legal assistance. Indiana Legal Help provides help with cases, downloadable forms, and access to no-cost or low-cost legal aid through IndianaLegalHelp.org. Three main services are available: Ask Legal Questions Online through Indiana.FreeLegalAnswers.org where you can ask legal questions confidentially and receive free answers from private attorneys (hosted by the American Bar Association, qualification required); Virtual Ask a Lawyer hosted by the Indianapolis Bar Association offering phone or online chat consultation; and Find a Lawyer through the Indiana State Bar Association directory to search for an attorney.
with: Court and clerk's office staff—whether or not they are licensed to practice law—cannot give legal advice because they have to stay neutral in every case.
Get help with your case, download forms, and find no-cost or low-cost legal aid.
Ask legal questions confidentially and get free answers from private attorneys.
Virtual Ask a Lawyer - Call or chat online.
Search for a lawyer using this directory.
without: An official website of the Indiana State Government
